title: URL References, Resolution, Identity Keys, Unicode, and Canonicalization · Transfer Build
summary: An independent site owner provides a different origin, URL space, markup fault, indexing policy, accessibility need, resource budget, dynamic boundary, and failure condition and transfers url references, resolution, identity keys, unicode, and canonicalization into a changed URL resolution and identity pipeline. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of URL resolution and identity pipeline with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Parse scheme, authority, userinfo, host, port, path, query, and fragment without treating a URL as a trustworthy string.
Resolve relative, scheme-relative, path-relative, query-only, and fragment-only references against the effective document base.
Remove fragments from network-fetch identity while preserving them when the stakeholder report needs document-location evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Url Component Model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab site-quality team handles case 8328 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile URL parsing, base resolution, fragment handling, conservative fetch identity, and Unicode host evidence; all network and parser work must remain below explicit budgets; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-unicode-idna-boundary-15ka96
# Competency: scraper-unicode-idna-boundary.
# Case 9144: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_unicode_idna_boundary_15ka96(references: int = 24, resolved: int = 24, fetch_keys: int = 18, unsafe_merges: int = 0, idna_failures_visible: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_15ka96 = "15ka96-69ak51-9144"
    assert evidence_variant_15ka96
    evidence_axes_15ka96 = (
        "authorization15ka96",
        "url9144",
        "response69ak51",
        "tree15ka969144",
        "frontier914415ka96",
        "report69ak519144",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_15ka96)) == 6
    if references < 1 or resolved < 0:
        return False, resolved, "url-count"
    if resolved != references:
        return False, resolved, "resolution-gap"
    if fetch_keys < 1 or fetch_keys > resolved:
        return False, fetch_keys, "identity-key"
    if unsafe_merges > 0 or idna_failures_visible < 1:
        return False, unsafe_merges, "url-security"
    assert resolved == references and unsafe_merges == 0
    return True, fetch_keys, "conservative-url-identity"
